title: Centaurea diania
taxonomy: Centaurea diania is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Centaurea, which includes many species of knapweed and related plants. The genus Centaurea is further classified under the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Centaurea diania within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Its classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family.

intro: Centaurea diania, also known as Dianthus centaurea, is a flowering plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wound healing, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea diania include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
